## The signal that makes this account interesting

**This is an active OSHA-backed, state-mandate readiness play with hard injury evidence.** On **2025-07-17**, OSHA reported a severe injury incident at this facility (OSHA ID 2025076958): cerebral and other intracranial hemorrhage without skull fracture—the injury profile consistent with workplace violence (assault, fall during violent restraint, or similar WPV-adjacent event). The incident occurred *after* SB240's 2024-09-01 effective date, which means Christus Spohn was already operating under the Texas mandate when the injury was reported.

SB240 requires documented workplace violence prevention programs, staff training, threat assessment, and emergency response protocols. The post-mandate injury evidence signals either: (a) existing program gaps Firefly can help close via detection and alert acceleration; or (b) an opportunity to upgrade the current incumbent solution with more integrated physical-safety (Ember + Lattice) coverage before the next audit or incident.

No facility-specific incident narrative surfaces in our engine beyond the OSHA report (Tier-B news research and Tier-C hand-research pending).

**Greenfield → mandate-readiness displacement.** Christus Spohn is operating under an active state mandate (SB240, in force since 2024-09-01) with documented post-mandate injury evidence. The facility is not a true greenfield, but the injury report creates a compliance-audit vulnerability: if the current solution did not prevent or adequately respond to the July 2025 incident, a champion will see tangible ROI in upgrading to Firefly's integrated detect-locate-alert-respond stack (Ember + Lattice) before the next state inspection or insurance audit.
